name: opencoven-first-party
description: Canonical Coven Cave marketplace packages with generated compatibility exports for Coven Code and other agent clients.

plugins.name: github
plugins.description: Repository, issue, and pull request context for implementation, review, release, and triage workflows.

github_token.title: GitHub Token
github_token.description: Personal access token used by the GitHub MCP server.

skill.description: Use GitHub context without leaking tokens or taking repository state changes without approval.
guardrails: Do not push, merge, close, label, or comment without explicit approval
Prefer read-only queries before mutations
Never reveal token values

plugins.name: gmail
plugins.description: Inbox reading, draft preparation, and email triage through Google Workspace.

google_workspace_credentials.title: Google Workspace Credentials
google_workspace_credentials.description: OAuth credentials or configured gws profile for Gmail access.

skill.description: Use Gmail for triage and drafting while keeping external sends behind explicit approval.
useCases: Extract action items into plans or tasks
guardrails: Do not send mail without explicit approval
Quote only what is needed
Treat email content as private by default

plugins.name: google-calendar
plugins.description: Calendar lookup, schedule reasoning, and event-draft preparation through Google Workspace.

google_workspace_credentials.title: Google Workspace Credentials
google_workspace_credentials.description: OAuth credentials or configured gws profile for Calendar access.

skill.description: Use calendar context for scheduling and planning without creating or changing events silently.
guardrails: Do not create, move, delete, or invite without explicit approval
Use concrete dates and time zones
Minimize disclosure of private event details

plugins.name: linear
plugins.description: Issue, project, and planning context from Linear's official remote MCP server.

skill.description: Use Linear issues and projects as planning context while keeping writes intentional.
useCases: Draft issue updates or task breakdowns
guardrails: Do not create or update issues without approval
Keep status reports dated and concrete
Avoid syncing private notes into public issue text

plugins.name: canva
plugins.description: Design search, creation, export, brand, and asset workflows through Canva's official remote MCP server.

skill.description: Use Canva for design work that needs brand-aware creation, search, edits, or exports.
guardrails: Do not publish or share externally without approval
Respect brand-kit and asset permissions
Confirm destructive edits before applying them

plugins.name: vercel
plugins.description: Project, deployment, docs, and log context through Vercel's official remote MCP server.

skill.description: Use Vercel deployment and docs context for debugging and release planning.
useCases: Look up Vercel docs from the official MCP server
guardrails: Do not promote, rollback, or change env vars without approval
Treat logs as potentially sensitive
Prefer project-scoped queries

plugins.name: asana
plugins.description: Task, project, and portfolio context from Asana's official remote MCP server.

asana_client_id.title: Asana Client ID
asana_client_id.description: OAuth client ID for Asana MCP V2 clients that need pre-registration.

asana_client_secret.title: Asana Client Secret
asana_client_secret.description: OAuth client secret for Asana MCP V2 clients that need pre-registration.

skill.description: Use Asana for work graph lookup, task summaries, and project status without silently changing assignments.
guardrails: Do not create, assign, complete, or delete tasks without approval
Keep work status concrete
Avoid copying private planning notes into shared workspaces

plugins.name: xurl
plugins.description: X/Twitter drafting, lookup, and approved-post workflows for Charm's social voice lane.

skill.description: Use xurl only for approved X/Twitter workflows and keep drafting separate from posting.
guardrails: Do not post, reply, like, repost, or delete without approval
Preserve exact approved text when posting
Record post IDs or failure reasons

plugins.name: filesystem
plugins.description: Scoped local file access through the MCP reference filesystem server.

filesystem_root.title: Allowed Filesystem Root
filesystem_root.description: Root directory the filesystem MCP server may access.

skill.description: Use filesystem MCP only with a narrow allowed root and explicit write intent.
guardrails: Use the smallest useful root
Do not delete files without approval
Do not expose secrets from local files

plugins.name: git
plugins.description: Repository history and diff operations through the MCP reference Git server.

git_repository.title: Git Repository
git_repository.description: Repository path the Git MCP server may inspect.

skill.description: Use Git MCP for repository understanding while preserving repo safety rules.
guardrails: Do not reset, clean, force-push, or commit through MCP without approval
Report dirty worktrees before changing state
Stage only files changed in the current session

plugins.name: fetch
plugins.description: Web content retrieval through the MCP reference Fetch server.

skill.description: Use Fetch for bounded source retrieval with primary-source preference.
guardrails: Prefer primary sources
Do not over-quote copyrighted text
Record URLs used in summaries

plugins.name: memory
plugins.description: Knowledge-graph memory through the MCP reference Memory server.

skill.description: Use Memory MCP for explicit, curated recall rather than dumping raw private context.
useCases: Maintain relationship between people, projects, and events
guardrails: Do not store secrets
Prefer concise facts over raw transcripts
Mark uncertainty when memory is inferred

plugins.name: sequential-thinking
plugins.description: Structured reasoning support through the MCP reference Sequential Thinking server.

skill.description: Use Sequential Thinking for complex reasoning, not as a substitute for evidence or tests.
guardrails: Do not present private reasoning as final output
Verify claims with tools or sources
Stop when the path is clear enough to act

plugins.name: time
plugins.description: Timezone conversion and current-time lookup through the MCP reference Time server.

local_timezone.title: Local Timezone
local_timezone.description: IANA timezone used as the default local timezone.

skill.description: Use Time MCP whenever relative dates, time zones, or scheduling precision matter.
guardrails: Use absolute dates when the user may be confused
Preserve the user's timezone preference
Do not schedule external events without approval
